title: Feature: hex ci — done-condition verification, ci command, CI workflow template

steps.description: Add `done_command: Option<String>` field to `WorkplanTask` in hex-core/src/domain/workplan.rs. This is the machine-runnable companion to `done_condition`. Deserializes from JSON; absent means documentation-only (backward compatible).
steps.done_condition: cargo check -p hex-core passes; WorkplanTask has done_command field; existing workplan JSON without done_command deserializes without error
steps.done_command: cargo check -p hex-core 2>&1 | grep -v warning | grep -c error | grep -q ^0

steps.description: Add `verify_done_condition(task: &WorkplanTask) -> Result<(), String>` to hex-nexus/src/orchestration/workplan_executor.rs. If task.done_command is Some, run it as a shell command. Non-zero exit returns Err with the condition text. Call this from run_step() after agent returns success. Persist result in gate_results.
steps.done_condition: workplan_executor compiles; a step with done_command that exits 1 is marked failed; a step with no done_command is marked completed regardless
steps.done_command: cargo check -p hex-nexus 2>&1 | grep -v warning | grep -c error | grep -q ^0

steps.description: Create hex-cli/src/commands/ci.rs with `pub async fn run() -> anyhow::Result<()>`. Runs 4 gates in sequence: (1) hex analyze via NexusClient /api/analyze, (2) hex enforce list via /api/enforce, (3) workplan done_command sweep — glob docs/workplans/*.json, find steps with done_command, run each, (4) spec coverage check — every step must reference >=1 spec ID. Print structured pass/fail summary. Exit non-zero if any gate fails.
steps.done_condition: hex ci exits 0 on clean workspace; exits 1 when a done_command fails; prints gate-by-gate summary
steps.done_command: hex ci --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'Run all hex enforcement gates'

steps.description: Wire `hex ci` into hex-cli/src/main.rs: add Ci variant to Commands enum, add `Commands::Ci => ci::run().await` match arm. Import ci module.
steps.done_condition: cargo build -p hex-cli succeeds; hex ci --help prints usage
steps.done_command: cargo check -p hex-cli 2>&1 | grep -v warning | grep -c error | grep -q ^0

steps.description: Create hex-cli/assets/ci/hex-ci.yml — GitHub Actions workflow template that runs `hex ci` on push and pull_request. Add CI template to hex-cli/src/assets.rs rust-embed Assets struct. Verify it is included in the binary with `hex init --list-assets | grep ci`.
steps.done_condition: hex-ci.yml exists in assets/ci/; cargo build -p hex-cli succeeds; asset is embedded
steps.done_command: cargo check -p hex-cli 2>&1 | grep -v warning | grep -c error | grep -q ^0

mergeOrder: step-1 first (domain change required by all)
step-2 and step-3 parallel (both depend only on step-1)
step-5 last (depends on step-4 for binary)

riskRegister.risk: done_command runs arbitrary shell — injection if workplan JSON is attacker-controlled
riskRegister.mitigation: Workplans are repo files committed by developers; same trust level as Makefile. Document that done_command must not include user-supplied data.

riskRegister.risk: False positive: done_command fails due to env difference (PATH, binary not on PATH)
riskRegister.mitigation: done_command should use relative paths or hex CLI commands. Document convention. Failures show the exact command + exit code so debugging is fast.

riskRegister.risk: hex ci adds latency to every CI push
riskRegister.mitigation: Gates are fast (analyze ~2s, enforce ~1s, done_command sweep ~1s per step). Total overhead ~10-30s acceptable for enforcement guarantee.

successCriteria: A workplan step with done_command that exits 1 is marked failed, not completed
hex ci exits 0 on a clean workspace with all done_commands passing
hex ci exits 1 and names the failing step when a done_command fails
hex-ci.yml is embedded in the hex-cli binary and deployable via hex init
Existing workplans without done_command field deserialize and execute without error
